messages.subject: [R-meta] Meta-Analysis in R - online course - 11-14 May
messages.subject_clean: [R-meta] Meta-Analysis in R - online course - 11-14 May
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
due to a last-minute cancellation, we have 1 seat is again available for the ?Meta-Analysis in R? course (11?14 May, online).

 
 
 
This hands-on training offers a practical introduction to evidence synthesis?from formulating research questions and conducting literature searches to effect size calculation, meta-analysis, meta-regression, and interpreting results. We place strong emphasis on heterogeneity and publication bias.
 
 
 
Sessions are fully practical, using R and widely adopted packages (e.g., metafor, orchaRd), with real datasets and reproducible workflows provided throughout.
 
 
 
More info & registration: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/metain-r/ ) 
 
 
 
Feel free to reply if you have any questions.
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )
 
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-meta] Update on CRAN Task View on MetaAnalysis
messages.subject_clean: [R-meta] Update on CRAN Task View on MetaAnalysis
messages.body_plain:
Just a reminder that the Task View is still growing. We now have more 
than 200 packages as the flow of new packages is still outpacing the 
rate at which packages are being archived.

-- 
Michael Dewey

messages.subject: [R-meta] Update on CRAN Task View on MetaAnalysis
messages.subject_clean: [R-meta] Update on CRAN Task View on MetaAnalysis
messages.body_plain:
Very delighted to know the Task View is still growing. I was concerning that the rise of gen AI tools is killing it as it has killed Stack Overflow. I personally have learnt a lot from the questions asked here and corresponding answers/responses (especially from Wolfgang and James).

Best,
Yefeng
________________________________
From: R-sig-meta-analysis <r-sig-meta-analysis-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Michael Dewey via R-sig-meta-analysis <r-sig-meta-analysis at r-project.org>
Sent: Monday, 18 May 2026 0:21
To: R Special Interest Group for Meta-Analysis <r-sig-meta-analysis at r-project.org>
Cc: Michael Dewey <lists at dewey.myzen.co.uk>
Subject: [R-meta] Update on CRAN Task View on MetaAnalysis

Just a reminder that the Task View is still growing. We now have more
than 200 packages as the flow of new packages is still outpacing the
rate at which packages are being archived.

--
Michael Dewey
